postfix_2026_07_10: SPLIT STATE. Current values from paradigm-2026-07-10-postfix.json (writing_email.gen, n=196; live node count 196 items). CLOSED IN BANK: recipient_title_share 84.69% (real 82.35%) + recipient_bad_count 0 (no more 'First Last' / bare-org addressees); scenario_words_mean 38.92 (real 39.45). PROMPT FIXED BUT BANK NOT REGENERATED (do NOT read as a live regression): subject_words_mean 6.74 / subject_ge8_count 50 (real 4.14 / 0), opener_you_other_verb 39.29% (real 0), verb_ask 24.49% (real 10%) / verb_inquire 0 (real 5.9%), verbs_all_distinct_share 1.0 (real 0.90). These move only when the bank is regenerated against the fixed emailWriting.js.
measurer: scripts/research/email_measure.mjs (all detectors hand-validated against the 51 real items)
notes: Real bank is recalled (reconstructed wording), but bullets are verbatim-faithful to the live exam: OCR of the actual writing screens (.codex-tmp/ocr/2.23/2.25/3.16/3.30/4.20 写作.txt) matches email.json bullets word-for-word. Scenario prose in email.json is lightly expanded vs the terse on-screen prompt, so trust bullet/recipient/subject structure more than exact scenario length. The on-screen task ALWAYS ends with the fixed instruction 'Write as much as you can and in complete sentences.' and the direction is ALWAYS 'Write an email to [recipient]. In your email, do the following:' — these are harness constants, not variables.

dimensions.name: bullet_count
examples: Every real item has exactly 3 bullets.

dimensions.name: bullet_lead_verb_distribution
dimensions.detector: first token of each bullet -> canonical verb (VERB_MAP); --dump verb
examples: Describe the issues you have encountered in the apartment.
Explain how conditions negatively affect your studies.
Request that he make arrangements to address these issues soon.
dimensions.gap: STILL OFF — bank pending regeneration. Ask still over-used (24.49% vs real 10%), Inquire still 0 (real 5.9%). maps_to: emailWriting.js:245 SLOT3_VERBS = ['Suggest','Suggest','Request','Request','Inquire','Ask'] now adds Inquire and demotes Ask; takes effect on regeneration.

dimensions.name: bullet_verb_by_slot
dimensions.unit: lead verb counts in bullet slot 1 / 2 / 3 (n=51)
examples: slot1 Describe -> slot2 Explain -> slot3 Suggest = the canonical 'describe situation / explain impact / suggest-or-request action' arc (e.g. gym item 2026-03-16)
softened variant: slot1 Thank/Mention-enjoyment -> slot2 Describe-problem -> slot3 Suggest (feedback emails)
dimensions.current: slot1 strongly Describe-led (matches); slot3 splits Suggest/Ask evenly — gen over-uses Ask in the action slot, real prefers Suggest/Request/Inquire there.
dimensions.gap: Mostly aligned. Real almost never uses 'Ask' in slot1 (0) and uses Inquire in slot3 (9, esp. for booking/inquiry items); gen has no Inquire at all and pushes Ask into slot3.

dimensions.name: distinct_verbs_per_item
dimensions.unit: share of items whose 3 bullets each start with a different verb
examples: ALLOWED repeat (real): 2026-01-27 = Describe / Describe / Explain — TWO 'Describe' bullets is authentic (5/51 real items repeat a verb).
2026-02-28 = Explain / Describe / Suggest (all distinct)
dimensions.current: postfix verbs_all_distinct_share 1.0 (real 0.90) — still rigidly all-distinct, bank pending regen
dimensions.gap: Minor. Gen enforces all-distinct 100%; real allows a repeated verb ~10% (esp. Describe/Describe). Prompt rule 'each starting with a DIFFERENT verb' is slightly too strict.

dimensions.name: macro_communicative_function
dimensions.unit: share of items by overall intent-triad (n_real=51)
dimensions.detector: classifyMacro(): blends bullet verbs + scenario keywords (issue/thanks/reqFix/suggest/askInfo/advice/planning); --dump macro. Hand-validated: ~3/51 boundary calls are fuzzy (appreciation vs advice).
examples: mixed-feedback: 2026-04-06 Cozy Corner — 'Describe what you enjoyed about your dining experience. / Explain the issues you faced with the service and your order. / Suggest ways to improve the service for future customers.'

planning: 2026-01-21-C Jasmine — 'Describe the ideas you have for the fundraising event. / Explain why you think these activities will be successful. / Suggest a time to meet and discuss the plans in more detail.'

advice-seeking: 2026-01-28 Alex — 'Describe to Alex what you have recently noticed about him. / Explain why it is important to maintain good health. / Suggest some specific strategies Alex can use to manage his stress and workload.'
dimensions.current: complaint+fix 29% (over) / info-request 20% (3x over) / planning 11% (3x under) / mixed-feedback 6.5% (3x under)
dimensions.gap: TOP-3 GAP. Real exam's signature blend = planning/coordination (31%) + mixed-feedback praise→problem→suggest (20%) make up HALF the bank; gen under-produces both (planning 11%, mixed-feedback 6.5%) and over-produces dry complaint+fix and pure information-request. Gen feels transactional; real feels relational+event-driven.

dimensions.name: recipient_form
dimensions.unit: share of items by recipient surface form (n=51)
examples: title+surname (82%): 'Mr. Thompson', 'Ms. Taylor', 'Dr. Smith', 'Professor Patel'
first-name-only (18%): 'Julia', 'Jasmine', 'Alex', 'Maria', 'John', 'Jessica'
COUNTER (never in real): full first+last name like gen's 'Linda Chen' / 'Marcus Rivera' / 'Gloria Vance'; bare org like gen's 'Customer Service' / 'Parks and Recreation Department' / 'IT Help Desk'
dimensions.gap: CLOSED — postfix title+surname 84.69% (real 82.35%), recipient_bad_count 0. The invented 'First Last' names and bare-org addressees are gone. maps_to: emailWriting.js:290 (prompt now forbids 'First Last' and bare role/org). Reflected in the bank.

dimensions.name: recipient_role_power_relationship
dimensions.unit: share of items by addressee power relationship (n=51)
dimensions.detector: recipientRole() — RECIPIENT-ANCHORED (reads the appositive role-noun bound to the recipient name in the scenario; does NOT fire on loose scenario keywords). Hand-validated: bakery-owner Ms. Lopez / yoga-instructor Ms. Martinez / hotel-staff Mr. Carter all correctly = staff, NOT professor. --dump role
examples: staff/service/authority (75%): gym manager Ms. Taylor, landlord Mr. Thompson, hotel manager Mr. Rodriguez, sound engineer Mr. Bridges, librarian Mr. Anderson, catering manager Ms. Johnson, restaurant owner Ms. Lee
peer (18%): friend Alex, friend Jasmine, group leader Julia, classmate Maria, classmate Jessica
professor/instructor (8% — RARE): Dr. Smith (group-project), academic advisor Professor Patel — only 4 of 51
dimensions.gap: Gen OVER-produces professor emails (16.5% vs real 8% — 2x) and slightly over-produces peer; under-produces the dominant staff/service/authority bucket (59% vs 75%). The real exam is overwhelmingly 'write to a service provider / venue / manager you don't know personally' — gen skews too campus-academic.

dimensions.name: scenario_opener
dimensions.unit: share of items by opening words (n=51)
dimensions.detector: opener(): regex on first words; --dump opener
examples: You are… (49%): 'You are a university student who has recently moved into a new apartment.'
You recently… (33%): 'You recently attended a university orchestra concert.'
Your [person]… (14%): 'Your professor, Dr. Smith, recently assigned a group project due in two weeks.'
You and your… (4%): 'You and your friend, Jasmine, are planning to host a fundraising event...'
dimensions.gap: STILL LEAKS the 5th opener — bank pending regeneration. Postfix opener_you_other_verb 39.29% (real 0). maps_to: emailWriting.js:228-232 four-opener block (You are 0.49 / You recently 0.33 / Your 0.14 / You and your 0.04) now replaces the old allowances; takes effect on regeneration.

dimensions.name: scenario_word_count
dimensions.unit: words in scenario (NOTE: recalled prose is lightly expanded vs on-screen prompt)
examples: 26w (short): 'Your professor, Dr. Smith, recently assigned a group project due in two weeks. You are frustrated because not all of your group members are contributing equally to the project.'

39w (median): 'You recently joined a student organization at your university that focuses on environmental conservation. The group is planning an upcoming event to raise awareness about recycling and reducing waste. You have some ideas...'
dimensions.gap: CLOSED — postfix mean 38.92 (real 39.45, was 42.4). Scenario length now matches real.

dimensions.name: scenario_sentence_count
examples: Real scenarios are typically 3-4 sentences (90%); 2-sentence scenarios are a minority (5/51).
dimensions.gap: Gen scenarios are SHORTER in sentence count (mean 2.5, mostly 2-3) while being LONGER in words — i.e. gen packs more into fewer, denser sentences. Real spreads the setup across 3-4 shorter sentences (role -> triggering event -> concrete detail). Caveat: recalled-prose artifact likely inflates real sentence count somewhat.

dimensions.name: bullet_word_length
examples: 5w (short): 'Apologize for the damage' (gen) / real short: 'Describe the jacket in detail.' (5w)
17w (long): 'Explain what part of Lisa's contribution you appreciated most and how her efforts helped the team succeed.'
dimensions.gap: Essentially matches (gen ~0.5w longer). No action needed.

dimensions.name: subject_word_count
examples: real subjects (short, plain): 'Damaged library book' (3w), 'Career Workshop' (2w), 'Resort Inquiry' (2w), 'Request for apartment repairs' (4w), 'Feedback on dining experience' (4w)
COUNTER (gen, too long/specific): 'Question about MEDAR 610 Final Project Requirements' (6w + course code), 'Defective keys and short battery life on order #AP-90217' (9w + order #)
dimensions.gap: STILL ~60% TOO LONG — bank pending regeneration. Postfix mean 6.74 (real 4.1), 50 items >=8 words (real 0). maps_to: emailWriting.js:295 (prompt now: subject = SHORT noun phrase of 2-5 words); takes effect on regeneration.

dimensions.name: scenario_specificity_quoted_name
dimensions.unit: share of scenarios with a quoted brand/place proper-noun in quotes ('Home Essentials', 'Coastal Retreat')
dimensions.detector: presence of quote char in scenario
examples: real: "a store called 'Home Essentials'", "a hotel called 'Coastal Retreat'", "a restaurant called 'Cozy Corner'", "a local gym called Fitness Zone"
Every real item includes at least one concrete anchor (a named place, a time, an event, or an object) even when not quoted.
dimensions.gap: Close (gen 24.5% vs real 29.4%). Both banks like a named brand/venue. Minor.

dimensions.name: topic_setting
dimensions.unit: share of items by scenario setting (n=51)
dimensions.detector: topicDomain(): mutually-exclusive precedence anchored on scenario's core noun; --dump topic. Hand-validated all 51 (events/workplace boundary on 'career fair/workshop' = the only fuzzy ~2-item edge).
examples: events/services/leisure (65%): gym equipment, hotel-stay feedback, restaurant feedback, concert sound, trip/party/retreat planning, print-shop inquiry, campus club event
academic/campus-study (20%): group-project (Dr. Smith), missed-lecture notes, internship follow-up, study/advisor balance
housing (only 4% — 2 items): apartment repairs (Mr. Thompson)
dimensions.current: PROMPT FIXED, BANK PENDING REGEN. Topic-setting share not re-measured in the postfix snapshot; the pre-fix bank was 6.5% events/services vs real 65%.
dimensions.gap: maps_to: EMAIL_CATEGORIES (emailWriting.js:218) recalibrated 2026-07-10 — a dominant 'G' Services & Events category at weight 0.55 (gyms/hotels/restaurants/concerts/trips/parties/campus events) was added and Workplace/Community cut. Takes effect on regeneration.

dimensions.name: recipient_topic_coherence
dimensions.unit: share of items where the recipient ORG plausibly owns the scenario problem (mismatch rate)
dimensions.detector: recipientCoherence(): scenarioDomainStrict vs recipientDomain cross-check; --dump coh. Validated 0 false positives after fixing 'moved-into-apartment + bought-product' confound.
examples: BUG (gen em79): tablet-warranty complaint addressed 'to: City Council Office'
BUG (gen em81): smart-thermostat Wi-Fi problem addressed 'to: Campus Dining Services'
BUG (gen em83): air-cooler malfunction addressed 'to: Hotel Reservations'
BUG (gen em85): blender warranty addressed 'to: Building Management'
BUG (gen em89): cutting-board engraving error addressed 'to: Library Services'
dimensions.gap: HARD DEFECT — real has ZERO. 5 gen items send a consumer/product complaint to a totally unrelated institution (city council, campus dining, hotel desk, building mgmt, library). These are auto-rejectable. Likely a generation bug where the 'to' field was filled from a category template decoupled from the scenario.

dimensions.name: dangling_reference_in_bullets
dimensions.unit: share of items whose bullet names a person/role never established in scenario+recipient (mismatch rate)
dimensions.detector: danglingRefs(): bullet 'Thank/Tell <Name>' or 'the <role>' not present in recipient, scenario, or direction; --dump dang. Validated: em104 ('the coordinator' established in direction) correctly NOT flagged.
examples: BUG (gen em80): recipient 'Student Housing Office' but bullet 'Thank Jordan for prompt attention' — Jordan never introduced
BUG (gen em90): bullet 'Thank Deanor for prompt attention' — 'Deanor' is not even a valid name and is undefined
BUG (gen em79): bullet 'Tell the coordinator...' but recipient is 'City Council Office' (no coordinator)
BUG (gen em89): bullet 'Thank the specialist...' but recipient is 'Library Services' (no specialist)
dimensions.gap: HARD DEFECT — real has ZERO. Real bullets always refer to the recipient by pronoun ('her'/'him') or by an already-named person. Gen leaks orphan names/roles ('Jordan', 'Deanor', 'the coordinator', 'the specialist') from a different template. Auto-rejectable.

dimensions.name: direction_line_format
dimensions.unit: fixed scaffolding string (constant, not a distribution)
dimensions.target: Write an email to [recipient]. In your email, do the following:
dimensions.detector: string equality on direction field; OCR cross-check .codex-tmp/ocr/*写作.txt
examples: OCR 3.30: 'Write an email to the IT manager, Mr. Evans. In your email, do the following:'
On-screen the bullets render as bullet points, and the screen also fixes To:/Subject: fields above the response box.
dimensions.gap: none — both use the canonical direction. NOTE: the live task also appends the fixed instruction 'Write as much as you can and in complete sentences.' which neither JSON stores (harness-level).

correlations.name: recipient_role <-> macro_function
correlations.finding: Role and intent co-vary tightly. PEER recipients -> overwhelmingly planning/coordination (9/16 planning items are peer) + advice-seeking. STAFF recipients -> complaint+request-fix (11/14) and mixed-feedback (8/10). PROFESSOR -> group-project complaint or appreciation. So 'who you write to' nearly determines 'what speech-act triad' the bullets form.
correlations.implication: When generating, pick role and function as a JOINT draw, not independently: peer => plan-an-event/ask-advice; service-manager => report-a-problem-then-suggest, or praise-then-critique-then-suggest.

correlations.name: first_bullet_positive_opener <-> mixed-feedback/appreciation triad
correlations.finding: When bullet 1 opens with Thank / Mention-enjoyment / Describe-what-you-enjoyed / Reiterate-interest (12/51 items), 10 of those 12 are mixed-feedback / appreciation / advice emails. The 'soften before the ask' move (open warm, then raise the problem, then suggest) is a recurring real-exam shape.
correlations.implication: The 20% mixed-feedback target should be implemented as: bullet1 = positive/relational, bullet2 = the problem (Describe/Explain), bullet3 = Suggest. Gen rarely builds this arc.

correlations.name: complaint+request-fix <-> staff recipient + venue/product setting
correlations.finding: complaint+request-fix items (14) almost always go to a staff/service recipient (11/14) about a venue or paid service (gym, hotel, internet, appliance, library book, catering). The fix verb in slot3 is Request (compensation/replacement/repair) or Suggest (improvement).
correlations.implication: Authenticity = the complaint is about a SERVICE/VENUE the writer is a customer/member of, addressed to its manager — not a corporate workplace process.

deferred.name: scenario exact length / sentence-count fidelity
deferred.reason: Real scenario PROSE in email.json is DeepSeek-expanded from a terser on-screen prompt (confirmed by comparing email.json to .codex-tmp/ocr/*写作.txt, where the on-screen scenario is noticeably shorter). So scenario word-count (D7) and sentence-count (D7b) targets are directional, not exact. To harden, OCR the scenario text for all 51 and re-measure against the literal on-screen wording.

deferred.name: register / tone gradient (deferential vs polite vs casual) as a measured ratio
deferred.reason: The PROMPT (scenario+bullets) never contains a sample email body, so register can only be inferred from recipient role, not measured from text. The recipient_role dimension is the measurable proxy. True register calibration belongs to the SCORING prompt (EMAIL_SYS_BASE power-relationship rules), and would need graded student responses to validate — not available in the prompt bank.

deferred.name: official-tier ground truth
deferred.reason: Only ~2 verifiable ETS-official email prompts exist publicly (poetry-magazine, Kevin-restaurant — see data/REFERENCE_BANKS.md / memory). All 51 here are recalled-tier. Bullet wording is verbatim-faithful per OCR, but distributions are 'real 2026 topics as recalled', not ETS-published. Treat ratios as strong signal, not gospel ground truth.

deferred.name: subject capitalization style (Title Case vs sentence case)
deferred.reason: Real mixes both ('Request for Lecture Notes' Title Case vs 'Feedback on dining experience' sentence case) seemingly at random across recalls — likely a recall/transcription artifact, not a controllable exam feature. Not worth a target.
